macro_context.market_sentiment: Broadly bullish on April 30: S&P 500 hit a new all-time high (+0.8%), Dow up 1.5% fueled by Caterpillar's 10% surge on earnings beat; Qualcomm +16% on strong results; AI-driven business investment accelerating. Some headwinds from Meta (-9%) and Microsoft (-5%) on elevated AI capex. JPM raised its own S&P 500 year-end target to 7,600.
macro_context.fed_outlook: FOMC held rates steady at 3.5-3.75% on April 29 — third consecutive pause, with an unusual 8-4 dissent split last seen in October 1992. Inflation remains above 2% target driven by tariff pass-through and energy prices; easing bias retained in statement but contested. Powell remains as Fed governor in 'low profile' role; Kevin Warsh awaits Senate confirmation as next Chair.
macro_context.geopolitical: US blockade of Iranian ports has lifted oil prices and heightened Middle East risk; IMF projects a moderate 19% energy commodity price increase in 2026. Strait of Hormuz closure risk flagged. Global growth slowed to 3.1%, inflation 4.4% (IMF). US tariffs and export controls creating trade policy uncertainty. NATO-Greenland tensions elevated.
key_events: FOMC next meeting (date TBD) — markets watching for first cut signal
Mega-cap earnings: Alphabet, Amazon, Meta (Apr 29 after-hours results now processing)
US GDP and inflation data releases in May
Kevin Warsh Senate confirmation as Fed Chair
Ongoing US-Iran geopolitical situation — oil price wildcard

ticker_news.headline: JPMorgan Q1 2026 EPS $5.94 beats $5.45 estimate; revenue $50.54B beats $49.17B on record Markets performance and strong IB revenue
JPMorgan raises S&P 500 year-end target to 7,600 from 7,200, citing AI momentum and stronger earnings
CEO Jamie Dimon warns credit downturn will be 'worse than people think'; flags potential bond market issues
JPMorgan CIO reveals $19.8B tech and AI budget for 2026 (+10% YoY), targeting 65,000+ technologists
JPMorgan exec faces lawsuit alleging sexual harassment of junior banker — reputational risk headline

analyst_consensus.recent_changes: Evercore ISI maintained Outperform rating and set price target of $340 on April 17, 2026. Broader consensus: 33 analysts covering JPM — 14 Buy, 12 Hold, 0 Sell. Median target $345, range $257.89–$420. No major downgrades this month post-Q1 beat. Sources: Benzinga, Barchart, MarketBeat.

web_context.competitor_activity: Goldman Sachs posted record Q1 equities trading revenue, outperforming estimates and competing directly with JPM in fixed income and investment banking. Morgan Stanley remains strong in wealth management. Bank of America is the key diversified rival. Dimon called out blockchain/stablecoin platforms as an emerging structural competitive threat to traditional banking. JPM maintains the largest US bank market cap and leads on technology investment at $19.8B.
web_context.earnings_info: Next earnings: July 14, 2026 (Q2 2026). Analyst EPS estimate: $5.36. Most recent report Q1 2026 (April 14, 2026): EPS $5.94 (beat by 8.2%), revenue $50.54B (beat by $1.37B). Sources: MarketBeat, Investing.com, Nasdaq.
